title: potentilla subcaulis
intro: Potentilla subcaulis, also known as the dwarf potentilla or creeping cinquefoil, is a low-growing, mat-forming perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's roots and stems have been used to treat various health conditions, such as digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, potentilla subcaulis is typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
